perturbation.original_text: FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its sole discretion, shall choose. FIIOC may change the format and/or content of such informational reports, and the frequency with which such information is provided.
perturbation.changed_text: FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its best judgement, shall decide with mutual written consent by both parties. FIIOC may change the format and/or content of such informational reports, and the frequency with which such information is provided.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'sole discretion' to 'best judgement' with mutual written consent by both parties, it creates an in-text contradiction. The initial agreement specifies that FIIOC has the authority to determine the content and format of the information provided to the Company, but the added clause requires mutual written consent. This implies that the company has to consent on the format and content of the information, which can create disagreement if there is no agreement on the content format, since the original text the FIIOC had the upper hand. The modified version introduces a mutual agreement requirement, conflicting with FIIOC's initial power.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information to be Provided to Company. FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its best judgement, shall decide with mutual written consent by both parties. FIIOC may change the format and/or content of such informational reports, and the frequency with which such information is provided. For purposes of Section 4.2 of each of the Company's Participation Agreement(s) with the Funds, FIIOC represents that it is the designee of the Funds, and Company may therefore use the information provided by FIIOC without seeking additional permission from the Funds.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'sole discretion' to 'best judgement' with mutual written consent directly contradicts the implied unilateral power of FIIOC. The Contradicted Text, being identical to the Changed Text, still contains this contradiction. The original text allowed FIIOC to decide on content and format independently, while the changed text introduces a requirement for mutual consent, creating a direct conflict regarding decision-making authority over the information provided, thus, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In recognition of the fact that Company will respond to inquiries that otherwise would be handled by FIIOC, FIIOC agrees to pay Company a quarterly fee computed as follows: At the close of each calendar quarter, FIIOC will determine the Average Daily Assets held in the Funds by the Company. Average Daily Assets shall be the sum of the daily assets for each calendar day in the quarter divided by the number of calendar days in the quarter. The Average Daily Assets shall be multiplied by [ ] ([ ] basis points) and that sum shall be divided by four. The resulting number shall be the quarterly fee for that quarter, which shall be paid to Company during the following month.
perturbation.changed_text: In recognition of the fact that Company will respond to inquiries that otherwise would be handled by FIIOC, FIIOC may offer some form of payment to Company a quarterly fee computed as follows: At the close of each calendar quarter, FIIOC will determine the Average Daily Assets held in the Funds by the Company. Average Daily Assets shall be the sum of the daily assets for each calendar day in the quarter divided by the number of calendar days in the quarter. The Average Daily Assets shall be multiplied by [ ] ([ ] basis points) and that sum shall be divided by four. The resulting number shall be the quarterly fee for that quarter, which shall be paid to Company during the following month.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'FIIOC agrees to pay' to 'FIIOC may offer some form of payment', introduces ambiguity and contradicts the subsequent detailed calculation of the 'quarterly fee.' The original text creates an obligation for FIIOC to pay a calculated fee. The modification makes the payment discretionary which is in-text contradictory, and it's unclear whether the calculated amount must actually be paid, and creates uncertainty about the payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation to Company. In recognition of the fact that Company will respond to inquiries that otherwise would be handled by FIIOC, FIIOC may offer some form of payment to Company a quarterly fee computed as follows: At the close of each calendar quarter, FIIOC will determine the Average Daily Assets held in the Funds by the Company. Average Daily Assets shall be the sum of the daily assets for each calendar day in the quarter divided by the number of calendar days in the quarter. The Average Daily Assets shall be multiplied by [ ] ([ ] basis points) and that sum shall be divided by four. The resulting number shall be the quarterly fee for that quarter, which shall be paid to Company during the following month. Should any Participation Agreement(s) between Company and any Fund(s) be terminated effective before the last day of a quarter, Company shall be entitled to a fee for that portion of the quarter during which the Participation Agreement was still in effect, unless such termination is due to misconduct on the part of the Company. For such a stub quarter, Average Daily Assets shall be the sum of the daily assets for each calendar day in the quarter through and including the date of termination of the Participation Agreement(s), divided by the number of calendar days in that quarter for which the Participation Agreement was in effect. Such Average Daily Assets shall be multiplied by [ ] ([ ] basis points) and that number shall be multiplied by the number of days in such quarter that the Participation Agreement was in effect, then divided by three hundred sixty-five. The resulting number shall be the quarterly fee for the stub quarter, which shall be paid to Company during the following month. Notwithstanding the foregoing, compensation for each calendar quarter will not exceed [ ].
perturbation.justification: The change from 'FIIOC agrees to pay' to 'FIIOC may offer some form of payment' introduces a significant ambiguity and contradiction. The original text establishes a clear obligation for FIIOC to pay the quarterly fee. The modified text makes the payment discretionary, contradicting the subsequent detailed calculation of the 'quarterly fee'. If the payment is discretionary, the calculation becomes meaningless, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty about the payment terms, rendering the 'Compensation to Company' section inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. The contradicted text repeats the same discretionary payment language, highlighting the pervasiveness of the contradiction within the document.
